steps.title: Read the concern
steps.content: Gibbon says he cannot describe the success of his work without betraying his vanity. He is worried that describing the rapid sales will make his pride visible. In context, the word must mean revealing something he would rather keep hidden.

steps.title: Reject unrelated meanings
steps.content: Choice A means abandoning, but Gibbon is not deserting his vanity. Choice B means misrepresenting, though the issue is disclosure. Choice C means using something for gain, which is not the concern. The word for making something private visible fits the sentence. The answer is D.
